DEFENDANT NESTLE PURINA PETCARE COMPANY's MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PAY TRANSFER COSTS AND FEES IS Granted. No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-22-2014 Continued To Oct-08-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Jun-16-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT NESTLE PURINA PETCARE COMPANY's Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venue
is Granted. This matter is transferred to Kern County. "In cases with mixed causes of action, a motion for change of venue must be granted on the entire complaint if the defendant is entitled to a change of venue on any one cause of action." (Brown v. Superior Court (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 488.) Plaintiff does not dispute that venue is not proper in San Francisco for Defendant Nestle Purina Petcare Co. ("Defendant"), but argues that Defendant's principal place of business is not located in Kern County. "A corporation is bound for venue purposes by its designation of its 'principal place of business' in corporate documents filed with the Secretary of State." (Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (Rutter Group 2013), par. 3:521.1.) This rule applies to foreign corporations. (Gutierrez v. Sup. Ct. (1966) 243 Cal.App.2d 710, 728.) Defendant designates its principal place of business in California as located in Kern County on its Statement of Information filed with the Secretary of State. (Declaration of Stacy Bacilek, Ex. A.) Where several venues would be proper, the court must transfer to the venue selected by the moving defendant. (Cubic Corp. v. Sup. Ct.(1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 622, 625.) Because Kern County is a proper venue and Defendant seeks to have the matter transferred there, this matter must be transferred to Kern County. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant reasonable fees incurred in filing this motion in the amount of $ 4,575.00 pursuant to CCP sec. 396b. Plaintiff offers no evidence in support of her arguments as to why she did not respond to Defendant's requests to stipulate to transferring venue. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2014 Continued To Aug-06-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar May-15-2014 Continued To Jun-16-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NESTLE PURINA PETCARE COMPANY's Re-Noticed Motion To Transfer Venue is continued to June 16, 2014 to give both parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendant Nestle Purina Petcare Company must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers, including the underlying moving papers previously filed on March 17, 2014 and Plaintiff Firenza Pini must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers filed May 5, 2014, to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 15, 2014 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the Re-Notice. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Apr-16-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT NESTLE PURINA PETCARE COMPANY's Motion To Transfer Venue is off calendar. The proof of service is defective because there is no page attached with a signature from the declarant. Presumably the moving party's courtesy copy forgot to attach the last page of the proof of service. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
